symptoms.symptom_name: Barking cough + hoarseness + stridor after a coryzal prodrome

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A seal-like barking cough with stridor after a cold is characteristic of croup.
A coryzal prodrome without a choking episode argues against a foreign body.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stridor only with agitation, absent at rest; no work of breathing at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stridor only on agitation with no work of breathing at rest defines mild croup.

symptoms.symptom_name: No drooling / can swallow saliva / not toxic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ability to swallow saliva, no drooling and a barking cough argue strongly against epiglottitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Croup is clinical; neck X-ray is not needed and can distress the child, delaying treatment.
